steps.description: Rust integration test for Lock flow
steps.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test sequence_lock -- --nocapture
steps.expected: Test exits with code 0; stdout confirms lock_id assigned, SR_0 computed via SHA3-256("QS_LOCK_V1" || chain_id || asset || amount || dest_addr || expiry || nonce || pk_dilithium), and lockWithSR0 transaction submitted to L1 Vault at 0x6F889C00a5e674ab0b9403AfBa0fBEbe30511c67 on Sepolia; no panic or FAILED line in output

steps.description: Playwright integration spec for Consumer Lock
steps.command: cd src/frontend/web && npx playwright test e2e/integration/lock.integration.spec.ts --reporter=json
steps.expected: All Playwright tests in lock.integration.spec.ts pass (status: 'passed' for every test in JSON output); UI lock creation flow reaches success state and a corresponding DB row is present; zero test failures reported

steps.description: Verify lock row was created and pk_dilithium populated
steps.command: psql "$DATABASE_URL" -t -A -c "SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;"
steps.expected: Exactly one row returned; status is either 'pending' or 'locked'; length(pk_dilithium) is exactly 1952 bytes (Dilithium public key size)

steps.description: Verify totalLocked increased on Sepolia Vault
steps.expected: Returned uint256 value is strictly greater than 0 wei, confirming at least one Locked event has been emitted by the L1 Vault and assets are held by the contract

acceptance_criteria: Backend integration test exits with code 0 and stdout confirms SR_0 computed and lockWithSR0 submitted — no FAILED or panic lines present
Playwright lock.integration.spec.ts reports zero failed tests and the JSON output contains status: 'passed' for every test case
The most recent row in the locks table has status = 'locked' and length(pk_dilithium) = 1952 exactly
Sepolia Vault totalLocked() returns a value strictly greater than 0 wei
No silent fallback or hardcoded address appears in any layer's trace or log output

layer_results.command: cd src/api/api && SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo test --test sequence_e2e_test sequence_lock -- --nocapture

quality_findings.title: Frontend passes all 40 tests but DB contains zero rows — lock is never persisted
quality_findings.detail: The frontend integration suite reports 'POST /v1/lock creates lock with valid SR_0 and lock_id' as passed and 'lock is persisted in DB and visible via GET /v1/explorer/locks' as passed, yet the DB layer query returned an empty result set (stdout_excerpt is empty, zero rows). This is a classic cross-layer silent failure: the mock/stub backend that the Playwright tests hit is returning well-formed 200 responses and fabricated lock_ids/SR_0s without ever writing to the real database. The backend Rust test suite failed (exit_code=1), which confirms the real backend path is broken; the frontend is testing against a test-double that diverges from the real implementation. This means the lock sequence produces a plausible-looking lock_id (e.g. '0x049904a89315a6cd24fa92c01cc47aae1537b68a4a527b333ce127421cdfc4d4') that has no corresponding DB row and will never be submitted to L1.
evidence: frontend stdout: '[Lock] lock_id=0x049904a89315a6cd24fa92c01cc47aae1537b68a4a527b333ce127421cdfc4d4' — test claimed success
frontend stdout: '[Lock Persisted] lock_id=0xc60c47a77d3f7b5d1c6af06907c1df480266083f0468c9b6fcebb1b6060eaeb0, status=active' — persistence test claimed success
db layer stdout_excerpt is empty (zero rows returned from: SELECT lock_id, status, length(pk_dilithium) FROM locks ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1)
backend exit_code=1, no test execution output captured — real backend is broken
frontend stats: expected=40, unexpected=0 — all 40 tests passed against a backend that produces no DB writes

quality_findings.title: Backend test failure root cause hidden by output truncation — lock write path completely unverified
quality_findings.detail: The backend Rust test (`sequence_lock`) exited with code 1 after a 120-second run, but the entire stderr excerpt (2507 bytes truncated) contains only dependency compilation lines with zero test execution output. The stdout_excerpt is empty. This means: (1) the actual panic/assertion/error that caused exit_code=1 is invisible; (2) no log evidence exists for lock_id assignment, SR_0 computation (SHA3-256(QS_LOCK_V1 || ...)), or the `lockWithSR0` call to the L1Vault. The 120-second duration (timeout threshold) strongly suggests the test hung or panicked early rather than completing normally, which is consistent with the DB showing zero rows. Without the actual error, the silent-failure patterns from items 3, 4, and 5 in the priority list (unwrap_or_default, hex_to_bytes32_or_zero, Ok(()) swallowing errors) cannot be ruled out.
evidence: backend exit_code=1, duration_ms=120018 (exactly 120 seconds — timeout boundary)
backend stderr_excerpt ends at: 'Compiling iana-time-zone v0.1.64' — no test output follows
backend stdout_excerpt is empty — no PASSED/FAILED/panicked lines captured
stderr note: '[truncated 2507 bytes]' — actual failure message was cut off

quality_findings.title: L1 totalLocked state unverifiable — lock sequence cannot be confirmed end-to-end
quality_findings.detail: The L1 verification step against the canonical vault address (0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260) returned HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org. Combined with the backend failure and the empty DB, there is no evidence that any `lockWithSR0` transaction was submitted to L1. The frontend test '[Explorer] totalLocks: 30 -> 31' only reflects the off-chain explorer counter, not the on-chain vault state. The sequence is therefore unverified at every real layer (backend, db, L1); only the frontend stub layer reports success.
evidence: l1 stderr: 'Error: HTTP error 522 with body: ... 522: Connection timed out' against cast call 0x07012aeF87C6E423c32F2f8eaF81762f63337260
frontend stdout: '[Explorer] totalLocks: 30 -> 31' — off-chain counter only, no on-chain confirmation
db stdout_excerpt empty — no lock row exists that could have triggered an L1 submission
l1 exit_code=1 — no totalLocked value retrieved

quality_findings.title: pk_dilithium column presence unverified — Dilithium key storage regression possible
quality_findings.detail: The DB query explicitly selects `length(pk_dilithium)` to verify the 1952-byte Dilithium public key is stored with each lock. Because the query returned zero rows, it is impossible to know whether the INSERT code path even attempts to populate this column. If the backend is silently inserting a row without the pk_dilithium field (e.g. via unwrap_or_default on key generation), the column would be NULL or zero-length and the cryptographic binding between the consumer's lock and their post-quantum key would be broken. This cannot be confirmed or denied from the current evidence.
evidence: db command included: 'length(pk_dilithium)' in SELECT — intended to verify 1952-byte key
db stdout_excerpt is empty — zero rows returned, pk_dilithium length never validated
backend produces no output — no log line confirming Dilithium key generation or storage

final_verdict.summary: The Consumer Lock sequence fails at the backend and db layers: the Rust integration test exited after exactly 120 s with no test output (likely a timeout/hang), and the locks table is empty confirming no write occurred. The frontend's 40/40 pass result is against a test-double that diverges from the real backend, making it misleading rather than a true signal. The L1 layer failure is an infrastructure outage (HTTP 522 from rpc.sepolia.org) and not a code defect — per override rule 2 this is downgraded to FIXABLE rather than BLOCKED. No fixer proposals were received, so the recommendation is review_required pending human diagnosis of the backend hang.
must_fix_before_merge: Backend Rust test hangs/panics at 120 s — root cause hidden by output truncation; actual error must be surfaced and resolved
Lock write path broken — zero rows in locks table after full sequence run; DB INSERT never executes or is silently swallowed
pk_dilithium column unverified — 1952-byte Dilithium key storage cannot be confirmed until backend writes a real row
Frontend integration tests target a stub/mock backend, not the real service — test isolation must be corrected so Playwright hits the real API
L1 RPC endpoint (rpc.sepolia.org) is down (HTTP 522) — switch to a reliable fallback RPC before retrying L1 verification
unresolved_questions: What is the actual panic/error inside the backend test? The 2507-byte stderr was truncated before any test output appeared — the real failure message is invisible.

Is the Playwright suite intentionally configured to hit a mock server, or is this an accidental misconfiguration? The 'lock is persisted in DB and visible via GET /v1/explorer/locks' test returned status=active yet the DB is empty — these cannot both be true against the same real backend.

Did the backend test timeout because of a missing environment variable (e.g. DATABASE_URL not set despite SQLX_OFFLINE=true), a network dependency on the Sepolia RPC that also timed out, or an internal deadlock?
